title: clitoria flagellaris
intro: Clitoria flagellaris, also known as Butterfly Pea or Asian Pigeonwings, is a flowering plant native to tropical Asia, characterized by its vibrant blue flowers and long, trailing stems. The leaves and flowers of Clitoria flagellaris have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, and has been found to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Clitoria flagellaris can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or capsule, and is also used as a natural food coloring, adding a vibrant blue hue to foods and beverages.
